ab: Herek no lahok kita a matatala^ i ancila^ haw?
ch: Sera:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Hay, icowa^ kita a matatala^ a masaso’araw?
ch: Onak:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: I picodadan kita a matatala^ haw? milu-in kita.
ch: Sera:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: tala soraratan aca, cilosid kako a tayraan.
ch: Onak:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Makapah a macerak ko sakola no niyaro’ niyam, tayni hato i kacerakan no hana^ a mikilim i takowanan!
ch: Sera:我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Talae:n aca i kacanglahan, tada halafin.
ch: Onak:那要等到春天，太久了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: Ano matira, piilisinan aca a tayni kiso.
ch: Sera:那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: Hayda! mihamham kako!
ch: Onak:好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Milicay ho, hacowa kiso a malinah a tala niyaro’?
ch: Sera:請問你何時要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: Malasingsi^ to kako ta taloma’.
ch: Onak:我成為老師了就回去。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Nawiro cai ka i tokay a malasingsi^?
ch: Sera:為甚麼不在都市當老師?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Ma’ilol kako to niyaro’, mangalay kako a paco’ay to niyaro’ niyam.
ch: Onak:因為我懷念我的故鄉，希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: Mafana’ kiso a mitenoon, cima ko pasifana’ay kisowanan?
ch: Onak:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Ci ama ako, o mafana;’ay a mitenoon ciira i niyaro’ no niyam.
ch: Sera:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Nawiro mangalay a minanam a mitenoon kiso?
ch: Onak:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: O serangawan no mita ko tenoon, mangalay kako a misarayray to serangawan.
ch: Sera:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: kaeso’ ’inipen kina eraw.
ch: Onak:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: Nisaerawan no mako yo!
ch: Sera:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: Watah ko fana’ iso, halafin a misaeraw haw?
ch: Onak:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Cai, cecay a lipay maherek to , cacaken ho ko hakhak ato hemay, ta diwadiw han a mipacamol to tomod, ta fa^cal to paroen i ’atomo.
ch: Sera:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
